meaning: adj.由碎片组成的,断断续续的
examples: There is only fragmentary evidence to support this theory.
Some fragmentary definitions of 1688 still suevive.
Some fragmentary definitions of 1688 still survive.
Behind Sollers, for instance, we can make out the fragmentary figures of three men.
Radiocarbon dating can also be difficult if the bones are very small or fragmentary.
